UserStory: As a user I want to log in with my phone so I can access my account
ExpectedBehavior: Enter a Ghana phone number (>=9 digits) enables Log In; tapping calls signInWithPhone -> Supabase OTP; navigates to auth/verify with phone param; failure shows an Alert.
AcceptanceCriteria: Log In button disabled while phone.length<9 or loading; Successful signInWithPhone triggers success haptic and router.push to /auth/verify with phone param; Failed signInWithPhone shows Alert('Error', message) and error haptic, stays on screen; footer 'Sign Up' link navigates to /register/phone
EdgeCases: Phone <9 digits keeps button disabled with no alert; Network/Supabase failure falls back to generic 'Failed to send OTP' message; Rapid double-tap while loading guarded by disabled={loading}; No validation beyond digit length (any 9+ digit string passes, not Ghana-specific)
TestSteps: 1) Launch app cold, land on /auth/phone 2) Enter 200000000 (9 digits) 3) Confirm Log In button becomes enabled 4) Tap Log In, observe 'Sending code...' state 5) Confirm navigation to /auth/verify with phone param 6) Retry with <9 digits to confirm button stays disabled
Notes: KnownConcerns: none obvious. Code confirms Sign Up footer link (router.replace to /register/phone) is a distinct entry point. Code confirms: disabled while loading/<9 digits (phone.tsx:83), success haptic+push to /auth/verify (30-32), Alert('Error', msg||'Failed to send OTP') on failure (35), Sign Up footer replace (39-42). Minor: the Alert.alert('Invalid Number') fallback at phone.tsx:20-23 is unreachable (button already disabled); validation is digit-count only, not Ghana-prefix-aware. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to enter the OTP so I can verify my number
ExpectedBehavior: 6-cell OTP grid auto-advances per digit; 6th digit auto-submits verifyOtp; wrong code clears grid + refocuses cell 0 with error Alert; success links device (if deviceId present), clears troski_signed_out flag, router.replace to (tabs).
AcceptanceCriteria: Entering a digit auto-focuses next cell and the 6th digit auto-calls verifyOtp; On success linkToDevice runs if deviceId set, signed-out flag cleared, router.replace('/(tabs)'); On failure Alert('Invalid Code',...) shown, OTP cleared, focus returns to cell 0; Backspace on an empty cell moves focus to and clears the previous cell
EdgeCases: Verifying with no network shows Alert, no crash; App backgrounded mid-OTP keeps local 60s timer counting (not synced to server expiry); deviceId not yet resolved when OTP completes skips linkToDevice silently; No lockout after repeated wrong-code attempts (unlike wallet PIN's 5-attempt lock)
TestSteps: 1) From /auth/phone request a code for +233200000000 2) On /auth/verify enter 123456 digit by digit 3) Confirm auto-advance between cells and auto-submit on 6th digit 4) Confirm redirect to (tabs) home 5) Repeat with a wrong code (e.g. 111111) to confirm Alert + grid clears + refocus to cell 0
Notes: KnownConcerns: none. All other criteria confirmed in code: auto-advance (verify.tsx:41-44), auto-submit on 6th digit (46-49), troski_signed_out cleared + router.replace('/(tabs)') (56-58), failure Alert + grid clear + refocus cell 0 (60-63), backspace focus-back (68-74). The linkToDevice call itself is present but its guard makes it dead on the fresh-login path. | Verified: code-walk | Fixed+retested 2026-07-04 (code-verified, typecheck clean); runtime retest pending (needs device login / PIN cycle / live purchase)

UserStory: As a user I want to resend the code if not received
ExpectedBehavior: 60s countdown then Resend link re-calls signInWithPhone(phone) and resets the timer to 60 (current code: onPress={async () => { await signInWithPhone(phone); setTimer(60) }}).
AcceptanceCriteria: Resend link only appears once the 60s countdown reaches 0; Tapping Resend re-triggers signInWithPhone and resets the visible timer to 60; No loading/disabled state while the resend request is in flight (fire-and-forget)
EdgeCases: Rapid repeated taps on Resend before the timer visibly resets could send multiple OTP requests; Resend failure is not surfaced to the user (no Alert on error); Timer is local-only, not synced to actual server-side OTP expiry
TestSteps: 1) On /auth/verify wait for the countdown to reach 0:00 2) Tap Resend 3) Confirm timer resets to 1:00 4) Confirm a fresh code (123456 in test env) verifies successfully
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): Resend previously reset the UI countdown but never actually re-called signInWithPhone, so no new OTP code was sent to the user despite the timer resetting.
Notes: KnownConcerns: BUG originally reported here; current code confirms signInWithPhone is now called on Resend, matching the fix. Code confirms Resend calls signInWithPhone(phone) then setTimer(60) (auth/verify.tsx:124). Still no error surfacing on resend failure and no in-flight disable — both already-tracked edge cases hold. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to fix a wrong number
ExpectedBehavior: Both the header back arrow and the 'Change number?' link call router.back(), returning to auth/phone.
AcceptanceCriteria: Tapping 'Change number?' calls router.back(); Header back arrow also calls router.back(); Returning to auth/phone is a fresh mount with no stale OTP state carried over
EdgeCases: Back navigation when the phone param is missing/empty falls back to a bare '+233' in the displayed subtitle; Rapid repeated taps on back could theoretically double-pop the stack (not guarded)
TestSteps: 1) On /auth/verify tap 'Change number?' 2) Confirm navigation back to /auth/phone 3) Re-enter a number and resend to confirm the flow restarts cleanly
Notes: KnownConcerns: none. Both back paths call router.back() (auth/verify.tsx:81, 93). Missing phone param renders '+233' bare in subtitle (line 34) as tracked. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to sign up with phone+email
ExpectedBehavior: Phone>=9 digits + optional email enable Continue; malformed non-empty email is rejected before submit; on submit calls signInWithPhone then navigates to register/verify carrying phone+email.
AcceptanceCriteria: Continue disabled until phone.length>=9; Non-empty malformed email blocked with Alert('Invalid Email',...) before any network call; Empty email is allowed (optional field); Successful send navigates to /register/verify with phone & email params
EdgeCases: Whitespace-only email is treated as empty via trim() check; OTP send failure shows Alert('Error',...) and stays on screen; Continue tap guarded against double-submit via disabled={loading}
TestSteps: 1) Open /register/phone 2) Enter 200000000 3) Enter invalid email 'test@' and tap Continue, expect an Invalid Email alert 4) Clear the email field, tap Continue 5) Confirm navigation to /register/verify
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): email field previously had no validation at all — empty or malformed addresses were both silently accepted.
Notes: KnownConcerns: email validation bug now fixed with a regex check before submit; still permissive (accepts any syntactically valid address, no verification email sent). Code confirms: phone>=9 gate (register/phone.tsx:23,46), email regex on non-empty trimmed value (27-30), Alert('Invalid Email') pre-network, success push to /register/verify with phone+email (39), disabled={loading||!canContinue} (107). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to verify my phone
ExpectedBehavior: Same 6-cell OTP grid as auth/verify; auto-submit on the 6th digit AND an explicit Verify button now wired via submitOtp with a loading guard; success -> register/profile carrying phone+email.
AcceptanceCriteria: Verify button disabled while loading or any cell is empty; submitOtp is guarded against double-submit via an internal loading check; Success routes to /register/profile with phone & email params; Failure clears the OTP grid and refocuses cell 0 with an Alert
EdgeCases: Typing the 6th digit while also tapping Verify is protected by the loading guard so verifyOtp isn't called twice; Resend link on this screen re-triggers signInWithPhone identically to A3; No lockout on repeated wrong-code attempts
TestSteps: 1) From register/phone continue to register/verify 2) Enter 123456 and confirm auto-submit navigates to register/profile 3) Retry the flow, type only 5 digits (Verify stays disabled), then complete the 6th digit to confirm the submit path fires once
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): Verify button was previously a dead no-op (onPress was empty) — only the auto-submit-on-6th-digit path actually verified the code.
Notes: KnownConcerns: Verify button now wired to submitOtp with a loading guard; confirmed no double verifyOtp calls. Code confirms: submitOtp loading+length guard (register/verify.tsx:50), Verify button wired to submitOtp and disabled while loading/any-cell-empty (135-136), failure clears grid + refocuses cell 0 (62-63). Minor: success uses router.push (58) not replace, so back gesture can return to the spent OTP screen. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to enter my name/gender/city/referral
ExpectedBehavior: First+last name required to enable Continue; gender/city/referral are optional free-text fields (gender is a plain TextInput despite the 'Select gender' placeholder, not a picker).
AcceptanceCriteria: Continue disabled unless trimmed firstName and lastName are both non-empty; Gender/City/Referral accept any free text and are fully optional; Continue navigates to /register/review carrying all 5 fields plus phone/email
EdgeCases: Gender field allows arbitrary free text since it's a TextInput, not a picker — a real data-quality risk for a field expected to be categorical; Whitespace-only names fail the trim check and keep Continue disabled; No max length cap on any of the name fields
TestSteps: 1) On register/profile enter First 'Kwame' 2) Enter Last 'Mensah' 3) Leave gender/city/referral blank 4) Tap Continue and confirm navigation to /register/review with the correct values shown
Notes: KnownConcerns: Gender is labelled 'Select gender' but is actually a free TextInput (no picker) — should be flagged for a future UI fix. Code confirms trim-gate on first+last name (register/profile.tsx:24), all 5 fields + phone/email passed to review (29-32). Gender remains a free TextInput with 'Select gender' placeholder (57) — data-quality flag stands; no maxLength on name fields. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to confirm details before saving
ExpectedBehavior: Shows email/phone/city; Confirm upserts contributor_profiles (deletes any stale row on the same phone tied to a different device, then updates the current device's row); on DB error shows an Alert and stays on screen (no longer silently advances); success -> register/pin. Terms/Privacy links wired.
AcceptanceCriteria: Confirm button shows 'Saving...' and is disabled while saving; On a Supabase update error, Alert('Could not save profile',...) is shown and the screen does NOT advance; On success, haptic + router.push('/register/pin'); Terms & Privacy Policy inline links open /terms and /privacy respectively
EdgeCases: No deviceId available means the update is skipped as a no-op but the flow still proceeds to /register/pin (silent data-loss gap); Phone tied to a stale different device_id row is deleted before the update; 'Edit Details' uses router.back() rather than resubmitting any changed params
TestSteps: 1) On register/review confirm the displayed email/phone/city 2) Tap 'Terms & Condition' link, confirm /terms opens, then go back 3) Tap Confirm & Continue 4) Confirm navigation to /register/pin 5) If reachable, simulate a DB error to confirm the Alert blocks advancement
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): a failed Supabase save was previously swallowed — the user advanced to the PIN step as if the profile had saved successfully even when the write failed.
Notes: KnownConcerns: save-error swallow bug fixed (now throws -> Alert -> return, no advance); Terms/Privacy links confirmed wired. Code confirms: stale same-phone/other-device row deleted then current device row updated (review.tsx:47-55), throw -> Alert('Could not save profile') -> return with no advance (54-61), 'Saving...' disabled state (98-100), Terms/Privacy links push /terms and /privacy (112). No-deviceId still silently skips the update yet proceeds to /register/pin (52-66) — known gap stands. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to set a 4-digit PIN
ExpectedBehavior: 4-digit create->confirm flow; mismatch resets to the create step with an error message; PIN stored in AsyncStorage under 'troski_user_pin'; success navigates to register/survey.
AcceptanceCriteria: Entering 4 digits in the create step auto-advances to the confirm step; Matching confirm PIN stores to AsyncStorage and navigates to /register/survey; Mismatched PIN shows \PINs don't match. Try again.\" and resets both steps; Back on the confirm step returns to create step (clearing both); back on create step calls router.back()"
EdgeCases: Registration PIN is stored in AsyncStorage in PLAIN TEXT versus the wallet PIN which uses SecureStore — an inconsistent, less-secure pattern; Hidden TextInput relies entirely on the system keyboard with only dot-fill as visual feedback; Rapid re-entry during the 300-500ms step-transition setTimeout could double-fire haptics
TestSteps: 1) On register/pin enter 1234 2) Confirm auto-advance to the Confirm step 3) Enter 1234 again 4) Confirm navigation to /register/survey 5) Repeat with a mismatched confirm PIN (e.g. 4321) to verify the error message and reset to the create step
Notes: KnownConcerns: Registration PIN stored in AsyncStorage (plain text) vs wallet PIN in SecureStore — inconsistent/insecure; should be unified onto SecureStore. Flow criteria all confirmed (auto-advance register/pin.tsx:38-40, match->store->survey 47-51, mismatch error+reset 52-55, back handling 68-72). NEW: 'troski_user_pin' is WRITE-ONLY — grep shows no reader anywhere in app/ or lib/ (only writer pin.tsx:13,49), so the subtitle promise 'You'll use this to sign in and approve transactions' is false; wallet PIN is a separate SecureStore system (lib/services/walletPin.ts). Plain-text AsyncStorage concern stands; the step collects a credential the app never uses. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to say how I found Troski
ExpectedBehavior: Radio options set referral_source; 'Go to Home Screen' best-effort writes referral_source, then calls completeOnboarding, then router.replace to (tabs); a submitting guard + disabled button prevent double completeOnboarding calls.
AcceptanceCriteria: Selecting an option highlights its radio + updates label styling; Done button shows 'Setting up…' and is disabled while submitting; referral_source write is attempted only if an option was selected, and failures are caught/logged without blocking; Flow always ends in completeOnboarding() + router.replace('/(tabs)') regardless of the write outcome
EdgeCases: No option selected still allows proceeding (Done works with selected=null); Supabase update failure for referral_source is swallowed via console.warn only — silent data loss on a low-value field; Double-tap on Done is guarded by the submitting state
TestSteps: 1) On register/survey select 'Friend or family' 2) Tap 'Go to Home Screen' 3) Confirm a brief 'Setting up…' state then landing on (tabs) home 4) Log out and back in to confirm onboarding does not re-appear
Notes: KnownConcerns: added double-submit guard + loading state; referral_source write remains best-effort/low-value and can silently fail. Code confirms: submitting guard + disabled + 'Setting up…' (survey.tsx:32-34,89-91), referral_source written only when selected and only when an auth user exists (37-44), completeOnboarding + replace always run (46-48). Note: supabase update errors return an error object (not thrown), so the catch/console.warn never fires for DB failures — write is fully silent best-effort, and it targets auth_user_id not device_id. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a new user I want to resend my signup verification code if I didn't receive it
ExpectedBehavior: 60s countdown then Resend link re-calls signInWithPhone(phone) and resets the timer to 60 — identical pattern to auth/verify.tsx (A3), but never independently tracked for the registration route.
AcceptanceCriteria: Resend link only appears once the 60s countdown reaches 0; Tapping Resend re-triggers signInWithPhone and resets the visible timer to 60; Behaves identically to the auth/verify.tsx Resend (A3, already fixed there)
EdgeCases: Rapid repeated taps before the timer visibly resets could send multiple OTP requests; Resend failure is not surfaced to the user (no Alert on error); Never explicitly regression-tested on this specific route even though A3 caught a real bug in the sibling screen
TestSteps: 1) Start signup at /register/phone with a fresh number 2) On /register/verify wait for the countdown to reach 0:00 3) Tap Resend 4) Confirm the timer resets to 1:00 and a fresh code (123456 in test env) verifies successfully
Notes: GAP: register/verify.tsx has its own Resend implementation that mirrors auth/verify.tsx (A3) but was never separately tracked; code inspection shows it already correctly calls signInWithPhone, but no dedicated test pass exists for this route. Code-walk confirms register/verify.tsx:124 Resend calls signInWithPhone(phone||'') then setTimer(60) — identical to the fixed A3 pattern. Same residual gaps: no Alert on resend failure, no in-flight guard against rapid taps, local-only timer. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to see my own profile (avatar/level/streak/bio/home route)
ExpectedBehavior: InitialsAvatar with a level-colored ring, streak Flame badge (only if current_streak>0), level pill w/ emoji, bio + home route line (if present); loads real points/streak via useApp/getOrCreateProfile.
AcceptanceCriteria: Avatar ring color matches LEVELS[current_level].color; Streak flame badge renders only when current_streak > 0; Bio card renders only if profile.bio or profile.home_route_label is present; No error Alert/toast on normal load, and Points/Streak stat values reflect real Supabase data
EdgeCases: profile null/undefined on first mount before AppContext resolves falls back to 'Commuter' + passenger level without crashing; deviceId not yet available is now guarded (won't throw a raw TypeError); Long bio text wraps within the bio card with only default text wrapping (no overflow handling)
TestSteps: 1) Sign in as +233200000000 / OTP 123456 2) Navigate to the Profile tab 3) Confirm avatar, level pill, streak flame (if streak>0), and Points/Streak values render with no error toast 4) Force-quit and relaunch quickly to hit the early-mount race and confirm no crash/error toast
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): getOrCreateProfile previously threw a raw TypeError on a transient/early-mount fetch (no deviceId guard or retry), surfacing an error toast and zeroing out stats even though the profile existed.
Notes: KnownConcerns: added a deviceId guard + 1 retry + warn-not-error; retested and confirmed it loads Points/Streak correctly with no toast. Code confirms: ring color from LEVELS[current_level].color ((tabs)/profile.tsx:30,56), Flame badge only when current_streak>0 (62-66), bio card conditional on bio/home_route_label (78-95), null-profile fallback to 'Commuter'/passenger (30,69). getOrCreateProfile guard+retry confirmed at lib/services/rewards.ts:18,24-35 (warn-not-error). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to tap my Followers/Following/Points/Streak
ExpectedBehavior: Followers/Following stat boxes navigate to /profile/followers?id={deviceId}&tab=followers|following (profile.tsx onPress handlers); Points/Streak boxes are plain Views (display-only, no onPress).
AcceptanceCriteria: Tapping the Followers box navigates with tab=followers; Tapping the Following box navigates with tab=following; Points and Streak boxes have no navigation attached (not TouchableOpacity)
EdgeCases: deviceId null is guarded via `deviceId &&` in the onPress so nothing happens (no crash, no navigation); Small tap target + uppercase textTransform on labels makes automated (Maestro) coordinate-based taps unreliable, requiring code-level verification instead
TestSteps: 1) On the Profile tab tap the Followers stat box 2) Confirm /profile/followers opens with the Followers tab pre-selected 3) Go back, tap the Following box 4) Confirm the Following tab is pre-selected 5) Tap the Points/Streak boxes and confirm no navigation occurs
Notes: KnownConcerns: navigation confirmed wired in code (profile.tsx lines ~100/107); UI coordinate-tap automation was inconclusive due to small target + text transform, so this was code-verified rather than Maestro-verified. Code confirms Followers/Following onPress with `deviceId &&` guard ((tabs)/profile.tsx:101,108) navigating to /profile/followers?id=...&tab=...; Points/Streak boxes are plain Views (113-120). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want my monthly transit spend
ExpectedBehavior: Renders a current-month summary (Trips/Spent/Avg) plus Top Routes using real transaction/booking data.
AcceptanceCriteria: Card renders Trips count, Spent total (GH₵), and Avg fare from real aggregated data, not mock; Top Routes list reflects the user's actual most-used routes for the current month; Component handles a user with zero trips gracefully (zero/empty state, no crash)
EdgeCases: New user with no bookings/transactions this month shows zero values rather than a blank screen or crash; Month-rollover boundary (e.g. testing on the 1st) should reset the summary to the new month; No explicit loading skeleton noted while the aggregation query runs
TestSteps: 1) On the Profile tab scroll to the Spending Summary card 2) Confirm it shows this month's Trips/Spent/Avg + Top Routes 3) Cross-check totals against wallet/transactions for the same period for consistency
Notes: KnownConcerns: none noted in the original audit. Code-walk of components/SpendingSummary.tsx: real aggregation from points_history reason='fare_report' joined to fare_reports (44-95), month-window + prev-month comparison, top-3 routes. Flags: (1) 'Spent' totals are self-reported fare reports, NOT wallet/booking transactions — the cross-check test step will not reconcile with wallet history; (2) zero-trip month renders NOTHING (early return at :62, component returns null at :121) — graceful but there is no zero/empty state card; (3) no loading skeleton (card pops in). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want quick links Notifications/Settings/Privacy/Help
ExpectedBehavior: Rows navigate: Notifications -> /activity (badge shows unreadCount), Settings -> /settings, Privacy -> /privacy; Help & Support opens mailto:support@troski.me (with a graceful catch if no mail client is available).
AcceptanceCriteria: Notifications row shows a red badge with unreadCount when >0; Settings row navigates to /settings; Privacy row navigates to /privacy; Help & Support opens the system mail composer addressed to support@troski.me, with .catch(()=>{}) swallowing failures silently
EdgeCases: No mail client installed on the device/simulator causes Linking.openURL to reject, caught silently with zero user feedback (dead-end UX); unreadCount undefined or 0 correctly hides the badge (badge != null && badge > 0 check)
TestSteps: 1) On the Profile tab note the Notifications badge count if any 2) Tap Notifications, confirm /activity opens 3) Go back, tap Settings, confirm /settings opens 4) Go back, tap Privacy, confirm /privacy opens 5) Tap Help & Support, confirm the mail composer attempts to open (or silently no-ops without a configured mail account)
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): Help & Support was previously mislabeled to open /terms instead of the support mail composer.
Notes: KnownConcerns: Help & Support mislabeling fixed; now correctly opens mailto:support@troski.me. Code confirms: Notifications -> /activity with unreadCount badge ((tabs)/profile.tsx:33, badge render 142-146; route exists at app/(tabs)/activity.tsx), Settings -> /settings (34), Privacy -> /privacy (35), Help & Support mailto:support@troski.me with silent .catch (36). Silent mail-client failure UX gap stands. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to edit profile / sign out
ExpectedBehavior: Header pencil icon -> /settings/edit-profile; Sign Out shows a confirm Alert -> supabase.auth.signOut() + AsyncStorage 'troski_signed_out'=true -> router.replace('/auth/phone').
AcceptanceCriteria: Edit (pencil) icon in the header navigates to /settings/edit-profile; Sign Out shows a native Alert with Cancel/Sign Out(destructive); Confirming calls supabase.auth.signOut(), sets the signed-out flag, and replaces route to /auth/phone; Cancel leaves the user on Profile unchanged
EdgeCases: supabase.auth.signOut() failures are not explicitly handled (no try/catch) — could leave the local signed-out flag set while a remote session lingers; No cancellation of other in-flight profile requests when signing out mid-navigation
TestSteps: 1) On the Profile tab tap the pencil edit icon, confirm /settings/edit-profile opens 2) Go back, tap Sign Out, tap Cancel, confirm no change 3) Tap Sign Out again, confirm the destructive option, tap Sign Out 4) Confirm redirect to /auth/phone 5) Re-login with +233200000000 / OTP 123456 to confirm the session was fully cleared
Notes: KnownConcerns: sign-out path verified earlier under SET5 (identical pattern); edit navigation confirmed wired. Code confirms pencil -> /settings/edit-profile ((tabs)/profile.tsx:46), Sign Out confirm Alert -> supabase.auth.signOut() + troski_signed_out flag + replace('/auth/phone') (156-172). signOut still has no error handling — caveat stands. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to view a user's profile
ExpectedBehavior: Avatar, level pill, meta (home route, join date), XP bar computed from real LEVELS tier bounds (was hardcoded thresholds), 2x2 stats grid (Reports/Streak/XP/Pulse), Followers/Following tap-through, Trophy Case badges, Follow button (hidden on own profile).
AcceptanceCriteria: XP progress bar % is computed from LEVELS[level].min_points/max_points with Math.max(1,...) guarding divide-by-zero, not hardcoded thresholds; Own profile (myDeviceId === profileDeviceId) hides the Follow button; Followers/Following stat rows navigate to /profile/followers with the correct tab param; Trophy Case section renders only when badges.length > 0
EdgeCases: Profile not found (bad/deleted deviceId) shows 'Profile not found' + Go back link, no crash; Top-tier user (max_points = Infinity) uses the isTopTier branch to show 'MAX'/100% instead of dividing by Infinity; isLoading state shows a centered spinner before data resolves
TestSteps: 1) From Pulse or Leaderboard, tap another user's avatar/name to open their profile 2) Confirm the XP bar and stats render without errors 3) Tap Follow, confirm the button state toggles 4) Tap Followers/Following stat, confirm followers.tsx opens for that profile 5) Open your own profile via a similar path and confirm no Follow button appears
ErrorsFound: BUG (fixed): the XP bar previously used a hardcoded threshold array instead of the real LEVELS tier bounds, producing an incorrect percentage/next-level mapping.
Notes: KnownConcerns: XP bar now uses real LEVELS bounds; retested and confirmed correct mapping. Code confirms: XP % from LEVELS min/max with Math.max(1,..) and isTopTier=!isFinite(max_points) branch showing MAX/100% (profile/[deviceId].tsx:59-66,120-122; LEVELS bounds real in lib/constants/rewards.ts), Follow button hidden when myDeviceId===profileDeviceId (56,88), Followers/Following tap-through (155-169), Trophy Case only when badges.length>0 (173), not-found + spinner states (34-51). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want my social graph
ExpectedBehavior: Two tabs (Followers/Following), paginated 20/page via a created_at keyset cursor, each row a UserRow + Follow button.
AcceptanceCriteria: Switching tabs resets cursor/users and refetches for the new relationship direction; 'Load more' appears only when more than 20 results exist and fetches the next page using the last created_at cursor; Viewer's own is_following state is correctly computed against the displayed list; Empty state text differs by tab ('No followers yet' vs 'Not following anyone yet')
EdgeCases: profileDeviceId missing from route params causes fetchList to no-op (guarded), leaving the list empty; Supabase query errors are silently swallowed in an empty catch block with no user-facing error state; No search/sort — large follower lists have no way to find a specific user
TestSteps: 1) Open a profile with followers 2) Tap the Followers stat to land on followers.tsx?tab=followers 3) Confirm the list loads and Follow buttons reflect the current relationship state 4) Switch to the Following tab, confirm it refetches 5) If >20 entries exist, tap Load more and confirm additional rows append
Notes: KnownConcerns: no search/sort on the follower/following list — a real usability gap for high-follower profiles, but functionally correct. Code confirms: tab switch resets cursor/users + refetch (followers.tsx:100-104), 21-fetch/20-show keyset pagination on created_at with lt cursor (34-50), viewer is_following computed per page (68-86), per-tab empty copy (140-142), missing id param no-ops (26). Errors still swallowed in empty catch (93-94); no search/sort. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want light/dark/system
ExpectedBehavior: 3 buttons (System/Light/Dark) render with an active highlight from prefs.theme, but handleThemeChange always calls Appearance.setColorScheme('light') regardless of the tapped option — dark mode is globally disabled by design (per CLAUDE.md).
AcceptanceCriteria: Tapping any of the 3 options calls Appearance.setColorScheme('light') (deliberate no-op for Dark/System); Active-option highlight is driven by prefs.theme even though the visual theme never actually changes; No crash or visual regression when tapping Dark or System
EdgeCases: prefs.theme can show 'active' on Dark/System while the rendered UI stays light — a visibly misleading state; No reconciliation between the stored preference and the always-light rendered theme
TestSteps: 1) Open Settings 2) Tap Dark, confirm the UI stays light (no visual change) 3) Tap System, confirm the same 4) Tap Light, confirm no crash
Notes: KnownConcerns: Theme toggle is a no-op by design; dark mode intentionally disabled app-wide per CLAUDE.md. Code confirms handleThemeChange ignores the tapped mode and always sets 'light' (settings/index.tsx:58-61) — deliberate per CLAUDE.md; active highlight driven by prefs.theme (140). Note prefs.theme is never updated by these buttons (no updatePref call), so the highlight stays on the stored default rather than the tapped option — misleading but by-design-adjacent. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a user I want to change language
ExpectedBehavior: 'App Language' row opens a native Alert listing all languages from useLanguage(); selecting one calls setLanguage(code), persisted via AsyncStorage; only 'critical' screens (per I1) actually render translated strings.

UserStory: As a commuter I want my ticket QR to work with no signal
ExpectedBehavior: The boarding QR renders from a local cache with no network call, with screen brightness boosted for scanning

EdgeCases: Cache is empty and the trip_code param matches nothing -> 'No active ticket' shown even if the backend actually has a valid active pass (known deferred gap); expo-brightness native module absent in a dev client that hasn't been rebuilt -> brightness boost is silently skipped; trip_code param doesn't match any cached pass -> falls back to passes[0], which could be the wrong ticket if multiple are cached
TestSteps: 1) Book a trip while online so the pass caches locally 2) Enable Airplane Mode 3) Navigate Wallet -> Active Pass (or wallet/tickets -> an active ticket) 4) Confirm the QR + trip code render with no network activity 5) On a rebuilt dev client, confirm screen brightness visibly increases 6) Disable Airplane Mode, confirm the same ticket still shows
ErrorsFound: If the local cache is empty and no trip_code param is supplied, the screen shows 'No active ticket' even when the backend has a valid active pass for the user — a real functional gap for a rider relying on this for offline boarding.
Notes: Cache-empty + no-param fallback needs a backend refetch-when-online path; brightness boost needs a native rebuild (expo-brightness). | Re-confirmed: getCachedPasses is cache-only ticket.tsx:39-44 — empty cache + no matching trip_code renders 'No active ticket' (:79-83) with no backend refetch even when online; unmatched param falls back to passes[0] (:41, wrong-ticket risk with multiple cached). Brightness boost optional-module probe :15-21/:48-65 skips silently pre-rebuild. ticketCache.ts:23 drops expired passes. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want a PIN required before the wallet spends money
ExpectedBehavior: First payment prompts PIN creation (stored securely); later payments verify against it, with a 5-attempt lockout and optional biometric
AcceptanceCriteria: hasWalletPin() checks SecureStore for an existing 4-digit PIN; if absent the modal walks create->confirm (walletPin.ts lines 14-21, PinModal.tsx lines 47-57); verifyWalletPin() does a plain string match; 5 wrong attempts flips phase to 'locked' (PinModal.tsx lines 95-106, MAX_ATTEMPTS=5); biometric auto-prompts on the verify phase if enrolled+enabled (lines 55, 59-62); entry auto-submits once 4 digits are typed (lines 113-120)

TestSteps: 1) On a first-ever booking checkout, confirm PinModal opens in 'create' phase 2) Enter 1234, confirm it advances to 'confirm' 3) Re-enter 1234, confirm the PIN saves and (if biometrics available on the simulator) an enrollment offer appears, else onSuccess fires immediately 4) On a later payment, confirm the 'verify' phase requests the PIN 5) Enter 1234 correctly, confirm success 6) Enter a wrong PIN 5 times in a row, confirm the 'Wallet locked' phase appears
Notes: Biometric needs a native rebuild (expo-local-authentication) and lazily falls back to PIN until then. | Re-verified create→confirm flow PinModal.tsx:74-93 (mismatch resets to 'create' :89-91), verify via verifyWalletPin :95-106, auto-submit at 4 digits :113-120, biometric auto-prompt :54-56 (needs native rebuild; 'Not now' never re-offers). UserStory: As a commuter I want to authorise a scan-to-pay payment with my PIN
ExpectedBehavior: A 6-digit PIN keypad authorises the scan payment and proceeds to a receipt
AcceptanceCriteria: Any 6 digits entered (no comparison against the user's real wallet PIN, no verifyWalletPin call) auto-navigates to /booking/processing after a 160ms delay (lines 20-27); this screen imports no service/fetch call at all — it never calls createBooking or any backend endpoint, so no debit and no real ticket are ever created; it reuses the generic booking/processing -> booking/receipt screens downstream, which can display an undebited 'paid' ticket
EdgeCases: User believes money was deducted after 'paying' via this flow, but no debit occurs and no server-side ticket record is created; any 6-digit sequence (e.g. 000000) is accepted as valid — the real wallet PIN stored via SecureStore (walletPin.ts) is never consulted; this is a second, inconsistent PIN implementation entirely separate from the real booking PIN gate (PinModal/W14) which does call verifyWalletPin
TestSteps: 1) Complete scan/index -> scan/confirm -> scan/pin 2) Enter any 6 digits (e.g. 123456) 3) Confirm it is accepted with zero validation and navigates straight to processing/receipt 4) Check the Wallet tab balance and transactions afterward, confirm NO debit occurred and NO new ticket exists — proving this flow is non-functional as a real payment
ErrorsFound: CRITICAL: scan-to-pay flow never calls createBooking (no debit, no real ticket) and the PIN entered is never validated against anything — any 6 digits succeed and the user is shown a fake success/receipt.
Notes: Confirmed in code: scan/pin.tsx has no fetch/service import; entirely disconnected from the real wallet debit + PIN verification path used by booking/checkout. | Re-confirmed CRITICAL exactly as filed: scan/pin.tsx accepts ANY 6 digits — next.length===6 auto-navigates to /booking/processing after 160ms (scan/pin.tsx:23-26, comment 'Any 6-digit PIN succeeds for now'); file imports no service/fetch (:1-7), never calls verifyWalletPin or createBooking → no PIN check, no debit, no server ticket, fake success receipt. Second inconsistent PIN UX (6-digit here vs real 4-digit PinModal/W14). Must be gated off or wired to PinModal + createBooking before release. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want to pick a transport service from Home
ExpectedBehavior: Horizontal scroll of 6 service tiles (Bus, Okada, Train, Pragya, Courier, EV) from SERVICES array; Bus -> /routes/search (live); Train -> router.push('/(tabs)/lines?tab=train') opening the Train segment of the Lines tab (live); Okada/Pragya/Courier/EV are comingSoon:true -> greyed 45% opacity image + grey text + "SOON" badge (absolute top-right pill) + Alert.alert('{label}', '{label} is coming soon!') on tap instead of navigating
AcceptanceCriteria: Only Bus and Train are live navigations; every comingSoon tile shows both the muted styling AND the SOON badge (visually consistent, not just text-only); EV specifically has a code comment noting it's built but intentionally gated until real GH charging data/EXPO_PUBLIC_OCM_KEY exists (do not silently re-enable route)
EdgeCases: Tapping a comingSoon tile mid-scroll (Alert still fires, no navigation); Train tile relies on lines.tsx reading the ?tab=train query param correctly — if that param parsing regresses, Train silently reopens Trotro Routes instead (this was the exact prior bug per old KnownConcerns); horizontal scroll clipping last tile on narrow devices
TestSteps: 1) Log in with +233200000000 / OTP 123456 2) On Home, scroll the Services row horizontally, confirm all 6 tiles render with images 3) Tap Bus, confirm navigation to Plan a Trip 4) Go back, tap Train, confirm the Lines tab opens directly on the Train segment (not Trotro Routes) 5) Tap Okada/Pragya/Courier/EV, confirm each shows a 'SOON' badge, greyed image/label, and an alert ('coming soon') instead of navigating

UserStory: As a passenger I want live trip tracking so I know where my trotro/train is
ExpectedBehavior: Idle state shows origin/destination (train: tappable station pickers; trotro: alight-stop picker) + 'Start GO Mode' gradient button; tapping it requests location permission if not granted (isPermissionGranted/requestPermission from useLocation), then calls startTrip(routeId, routeLabel, tripStations, {transportType, trainLineId})
AcceptanceCriteria: Button disabled and shows 'Starting...' while isStarting is true or tripStations is null; permission request happens inline (no separate settings deep-link) before starting; trip engine (lib/hooks/useTrip.ts) is a module-level singleton store so state persists across screens
EdgeCases: No GPS permission and user denies the prompt (handleStartTrip returns early, no error surfaced to user); tripStations fails to resolve (screen instead shows the 'GO Mode isn't available for this route yet' empty state before Start is ever reachable); route/line still loading (spinner state); backgrounding the app immediately after tapping Start (iOS CoreLocation pauses stationary watchers — see 8s poll fallback in useTrip.ts)
TestSteps: 1) Log in with +233200000000 / OTP 123456 2) Navigate to a route detail and enter GO Mode (or deep-link to /trip/[routeId]) 3) Confirm idle screen shows origin/destination + Start GO Mode button 4) Tap Start GO Mode, grant location permission when prompted 5) Confirm the map/sheet transitions to the active trip state with a progress bar
ErrorsFound: No graceful no-GPS fallback if permission is denied — handleStartTrip silently returns with no toast/explanation (app/trip/[routeId].tsx:315-318)
Notes: Confirmed: disabled+'Starting...' state ([routeId].tsx:1250-1261), inline requestPermission before startTrip (:315-317), useTrip module-singleton store (lib/hooks/useTrip.ts:103-112), 'GO Mode isn't available' empty state when tripStations null (:445-466); 8s poll fallback confirmed (useTrip.ts:222-231) | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a passenger I want to see my position + progress on the live map
ExpectedBehavior: Full Mapbox map (skipped entirely in Data Saver mode) renders route corridor (remaining + traveled line layers), pulsing vehicle marker (glow+ring CircleLayers), station dots (origin green/dest red/mid line-color), auto day/night style by Ghana hour; progress bar + stats row (remaining distance, stations passed, km/h) update from useTrip's progress; if user is >50km from the route (isNearRoute false), a simulated progress driven by schedule dwell times takes over so remote testers still see motion
AcceptanceCriteria: 'Saver' chip in the header toggles dataSaver state, swapping the full map for a lite gradient backdrop with route label + explainer text while keeping the bottom sheet's live progress; speedKmh computed from userLocation.speed (m/s->km/h, floored at 0 if unavailable/negative)
EdgeCases: Simulated progress mode used when far from the route — speedKmh always reads 0 here since it's derived from real GPS speed, not simulation (misleading km/h read while 'traveling' in sim mode); Mapbox tile/style load errors are not caught at this layer (could crash the map view); toggling Data Saver mid-trip swaps the entire render tree (perceptible flicker); no network for map tiles while dataSaver is off
TestSteps: 1) Start GO Mode per G1 2) Observe the map renders route corridor, vehicle marker, and station dots 3) Watch the progress bar and stats row (remaining distance/stations/km/h) update 4) Tap the 'Saver' chip and confirm the map is replaced by a lite backdrop while progress in the sheet keeps updating 5) If testing >50km from the route, confirm simulated progress advances and speed shows 0 while route position still animates
ErrorsFound: Simulated progress never sets a non-zero speedKmh (speed derived only from real GPS at app/trip/[routeId].tsx:266-272 — sim mode shows 0 km/h while marker moves); Data Saver toggle swaps entire render tree via ternary ([routeId].tsx:829) causing visible flicker mid-trip
Notes: Confirmed: Saver chip toggle (:1026-1035), lite gradient backdrop with explainer (:829-839), traveled/remaining line layers (:906-955), day/night style by Ghana hour (:474-478), sim progress when isNearRoute false (:487-548); Mapbox load errors uncaught at this layer as documented | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a passenger I want an alert near my stop so I don't miss it
ExpectedBehavior: When tripState is 'approaching', an amber banner renders in the sheet: 'Approaching destination — {formatDistance(effectiveDistToDest)}'; a haptic (Haptics.impactAsync Medium) fires once via hasApproachHaptic guard on entering the approaching state; a local notification is scheduled by the trip engine at the ~300m (trotro) / 500m (train) threshold per useTrip
AcceptanceCriteria: Banner only shows once per trip (state-gated, not re-fired); haptic fires exactly once (ref guard prevents repeats on re-renders); distance formatting goes through formatDistance() for consistent units
EdgeCases: App backgrounded/killed before the threshold is crossed (relies on the scheduled local notification firing independently of the foreground banner); GPS drift causing tripState to flip in/out of 'approaching' repeatedly (haptic guard prevents repeat haptics, but banner presence still toggles); simulated/remote-testing progress reaching the same distance threshold without real GPS proximity
TestSteps: 1) Start GO Mode per G1 on a trip already near its destination (or let simulated progress approach 100%) 2) Confirm the amber 'Approaching destination' banner appears with a distance value 3) Confirm a single haptic pulse fires (not repeated) 4) Background the app near the threshold and confirm a local notification still arrives
Notes: Confirmed: approach banner with formatDistance (:1052-1059), hasApproachHaptic ref guard fires haptic once (:302-306), get-off notification fired by engine at transport-aware threshold 300m trotro / 500m train + GPS accuracy (lib/services/trip.ts:30-33,148-150; useTrip.ts:152-170); background task path also notifies (useTrip.ts:396); note: notification fires AT threshold (trigger:null immediate), not pre-scheduled — backgrounded delivery depends on background location task being granted | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a passenger I want to report fare and earn points after a trip
ExpectedBehavior: On trip end (manual Finish Trip, or auto-end when tripState==='arrived' or simulated progress hits 100%), endTrip(deviceId) runs and on success shows a modal: trip summary (duration/distance/stations), a fare TextInput (GH₵, decimal-pad), Submit Fare (calls updateTripFare + awardPointsForTrip with withFare bonus) or Skip (awards base points only, fire-and-forget); reward result feeds ConfettiCelebration via setLastReward on level-up; after the modal closes (350ms delay to let the native Modal fully dismiss), router.replace to /booking/arrived with real distance/duration/stops params if reached_destination, else router.back()
AcceptanceCriteria: Double-end guarded by isEndingRef so the auto-end effect and manual Finish Trip can't both fire endTrip; Skip path still awards base points in the background without blocking navigation; offline trip end (no tripId returned) shows an 'saved offline — points awarded when connected' badge instead of the points badge
EdgeCases: Points award (awardPointsForTrip) is fire-and-forget with no retry queue — if the network call fails silently, points are simply lost; fare input accepts any decimal but there's no upper/lower bound validation visible in this screen (unlike RP1's fare report which is bounded 0.01-200); modal dismiss + navigate race relies on a fixed 350ms setTimeout, which could still race on slower devices; deviceId missing (handleEndTrip early-returns, no fare prompt at all)
TestSteps: 1) Complete or auto-end a GO Mode trip per G1-G3 2) Confirm the fare modal appears with trip summary stats 3) Enter a fare amount and tap Submit Fare, confirm points award and navigation to booking/arrived 4) Repeat and tap 'Skip for now' instead, confirm base points still award in background and navigation still proceeds
ErrorsFound: Points/fare award is fire-and-forget with no retry on network failure — awardPointsForTrip has no retry/queue (app/trip/[routeId].tsx:373-383,404-409); fare input validated only as fare > 0, no upper bound unlike RP1's 0.01-200 (:367-369)
Notes: Confirmed: isEndingRef double-end guard on manual (:331-342) and auto-end (:561-575), offline 'saved offline' badge when tripId null (:780-786), Skip awards base points in background (:394-410), 350ms modal-dismiss setTimeout race (:390,401), deviceId missing early-returns with no fare prompt (:332) | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As any user I want auth+push+onboarding+OTA handled globally so the app boots correctly every time
ExpectedBehavior: RootLayout loads fonts, wraps the app in GestureHandlerRootView > AppErrorBoundary > PersistQueryClientProvider > AppProvider > AuthProvider > AppInner; AppInner: shows native/TroskiSplash while fonts/onboarding load, runs OnboardingFlow if !completed (with register/login CTA driving OnboardingRedirect to /register/phone or /auth/phone), checks AsyncStorage 'troski_signed_out' flag to force a login redirect on explicit sign-out, forces light color scheme globally (Appearance.setColorScheme('light')), registers push notifications (usePushNotifications), commute alerts (useCommuteAlerts), daily check-in (useCheckin), watch sync (useWatchSync), and OTA update check (useAppUpdate, runs before splash even mounts) — plus a full Stack of ~50 named screens with per-screen animation/presentation options
AcceptanceCriteria: Splash sequencing avoids a 'home flash before login' by using a full-screen (non-modal) auth/phone stack screen with slide_from_bottom, not a modal that reveals content behind it; onboarding action ('register'/'login') skips TroskiSplash entirely and defers navigation one tick via setTimeout so the root navigator is mounted first; SplashScreen.hideAsync() is called both by TroskiSplash and as a safety net in AppInner so onboarding/signed-out paths (which skip TroskiSplash) can't leave the native splash stuck forever
EdgeCases: Fresh install (onboarding shows, no deviceId yet — usePushNotifications/useCommuteAlerts/useCheckin/useWatchSync all keyed off deviceId which may be empty momentarily); explicit sign-out (troski_signed_out flag true) racing with a stale auth session; OTA update available mid-session (useAppUpdate fires DeviceEventEmitter event consumed by OtaUpdateBanner, mounted at the ThemeProvider level so it survives navigation); font load failure (error thrown synchronously, no fallback UI, blank screen if useFonts errors)
TestSteps: 1) Cold-launch the app on the iOS simulator with no prior session, confirm TroskiSplash then onboarding (if first run) appears before any protected screen 2) Complete onboarding choosing 'Login', confirm redirect to /auth/phone (not tabs) 3) Log in with +233200000000 / OTP 123456, confirm landing on (tabs)/index without a home flash 4) From Settings, sign out, confirm 'troski_signed_out' forces a redirect back to /auth/phone on next launch 5) Trigger an OTA update (or simulate the ota-update-ready event) and confirm the OtaUpdateBanner appears above the tab bar without blocking the UI
Notes: Confirmed all ACs in app/_layout.tsx: troski_signed_out check (:112-120), forced light mode (:137-140), SplashScreen.hideAsync safety net (:146-148), OnboardingRedirect one-tick setTimeout replace (:93-97), auth/phone full-screen slide_from_bottom non-modal (:212), OtaUpdateBanner at ThemeProvider level (:242), useAppUpdate in RootLayout before splash (:250), font error thrown with no fallback UI (:266-268) — blank-screen-on-font-failure edge case still real | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a lost user I want a friendly 404 so I'm not stuck on a broken link/route

EdgeCases: Deep link or typo'd internal router.push route hitting an unmapped path; Link href="/" resolving to the (tabs) index vs. a raw '/' redirect ambiguity if tab layout changes; no back button/gesture affordance beyond the single Link and system back gesture
TestSteps: 1) From within the app, trigger an unmatched route (e.g. manually navigate to a nonexistent path via deep link or a typo'd router.push) 2) Confirm the 'Oops!' screen renders with the 'This screen doesn't exist.' message 3) Tap 'Go to home screen!' and confirm it returns to the Home tab
Notes: Confirmed app/+not-found.tsx: 'Oops!' Stack.Screen title, "This screen doesn't exist." text, Link href='/' (resolves to (tabs) index); plain unbranded layout — cosmetic gap vs design system | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a passenger I want to save mobile data during a live trip
ExpectedBehavior: 'Saver' chip in the glass header toggles dataSaver state (also settable via ?dataSaver=1 route param); when on, the full Mapbox map is replaced by a lite gradient screen showing the route label and an explainer, while the bottom sheet keeps showing live progress/stations/stats
AcceptanceCriteria: Chip visually fills/highlights (saverChipOn style) when active so the state is discoverable at a glance; toggling doesn't end or restart the trip — tripState/progress keep flowing from the same useTrip singleton
EdgeCases: Toggling mid-active-trip causes a full render swap (map <-> lite backdrop) with a visible flicker (see G2 concern); no persistence of the user's Data Saver preference across trips (resets to the dataSaverParam default each time); toggling while the map is mid-load could leave native map resources unreleased
TestSteps: 1) Start GO Mode per G1 2) Tap the 'Saver' chip in the header, confirm the full map is replaced by the lite gradient screen with route label 3) Confirm the bottom sheet still shows live progress/stats 4) Tap 'Saver' again to toggle back to the full map 5) End the trip and start a new one, confirm the toggle resets to off (not persisted)
Notes: Confirmed: dataSaver seeded from ?dataSaver=1 param only (app/trip/[routeId].tsx:101), Saver chip toggle with selection haptic (:1026-1035), saverChipOn filled style + 'Saver ON' label (:1032-1034,1377-1387), lite backdrop keeps sheet progress from same useTrip singleton (:829-839); persistence gap re-confirmed — no AsyncStorage/pref write anywhere for dataSaver, state resets to route-param default every mount; mid-trip toggle full-tree swap flicker tracked under G2 | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a rider I want to enter From/To and see ranked route options (direct, transfer, walk) so I can choose the fastest or cheapest way to travel
ExpectedBehavior: Service pills (Bus/Okada/Train/Pragya/Courier) filter transport mode; From/To connected inputs with swap; saved Home/Work/Current-location shortcuts; nearby-station suggestions sorted by distance; auto-search after both fields are filled with a 1.2s debounce via useRoutePlanner(from,to,mode); results render through <RoutePlannerResults/> with a walking estimate when both ends resolve to known station coords within 10km

Re-verified: 1.2s auto-search debounce search.tsx:190-202, swap :380, Home/Work/Current shortcuts fill+advance focus :278-288, walking estimate gated on FALLBACK_STATION_COORDS + <=10km :213-221, result tap pushes routes/detail with fare/duration/route_id/dropoff_order :513-525; empty state in RoutePlannerResults.tsx:54-57; Mapbox suggest + /api/addresses fetches swallow errors (:126-127,:162) as documented. Re-verified: 300m station snap pick-location.tsx:29,45-58; onMapIdle-driven reverse geocode :137-147; stale-response requestId guard :99-134; geocode failure falls back to lat/lng label :129-131; no-permission falls back to Accra center :25,78-96; Confirm disabled while geocoding or no coord :263; 'Set as start/destination' matches target param :271-273. UserStory: As a rider I want a rich map of my chosen route (live buses, ETA, stops, fare) so I can decide when and how to travel before booking
ExpectedBehavior: Full-screen Mapbox with day/night branded style, animated draw-in route line, origin/destination pins, floating ETA·distance pill, recenter FAB; bottom sheet with transport-mode meta, alight picker (for corridors with intermediate stops), fare with source label, live-trotro count, and two actions: 'Information' (Available Buses sheet) and 'Go Now' (-> booking/checkout)

TestSteps: 1) Log in with +233200000000, OTP 123456 2) Plan a trip Circle -> Taifa, tap the top result 3) Confirm the map draws the route line with an animated sweep 4) Confirm the ETA·distance pill floats above the sheet 5) Drag the bottom sheet up -> confirm the pill/FAB hide once the sheet covers them 6) Tap 'Go Now' -> confirm it opens booking/checkout (not a GO-mode trip screen) with the fare carried over
Notes: GO Mode is intentionally NOT reachable from this screen per an explicit owner directive (CLAUDE.md, 2026-06-12) — do not treat its absence here as a bug. EdgeCases: No bus assigned to this route yet (honest 'Assigning your bus' placeholder, not a fake driver); a bus assigned but not yet broadcasting live (shows 'Assigned'/'On shift', not 'Live'); balance fetch times out/fails (walletBalance stays null, Pay stays disabled indefinitely rather than allowing an unchecked pay); offline — balance fetch fails silently and Pay never enables, which is the safe failure mode but gives no retry affordance to the user

Notes: IMPORTANT CONTEXT UPDATE: as of this audit, checkout.tsx no longer shows hardcoded mock bay/departs/seats/bus-code/driver data — it is wired to real backend services (lib/services/fleet.ts fetchAssignedVehicle, lib/services/driver-ratings.ts). The GH₵0.25 service fee (SERVICE_FEE_INCL) is a hardcoded client constant, not fetched from the backend — should be verified against whatever the backend actually charges. Re-verified degradation path end-to-end: fetchAssignedVehicle (lib/services/fleet.ts:21-53) returns null on no-match/error -> checkout.tsx:331-335 renders the honest 'Assigning your bus' placeholder (no mock names anywhere); plate-only shows 'Assigned', live broadcast flips to 'Live' (:310-328); driver card is a TouchableOpacity only when a real driverName exists (:291-294); driver stats/reviews fetches are .catch(()=>{}) leaving honest defaults (:67-75). Pay gating: waitingBalance disables the button with 'Checking balance…' (:154,:411-415); failed balance fetch leaves walletBalance null -> Pay stays enabled-but... note: if the balance fetch THROWS, balanceLoading becomes false and walletBalance stays null, so insufficient=false and Pay ENABLES — the backend insufficient_balance check is the real guard (safe, single atomic call), but the client-side 'never enable unchecked' claim is only true while loading. Shortfall CTA rounds up and pre-fills wallet/fund (:391-406). Runtime: GET /api/wallet/balance returned balance 19.66 GHS + booking debits incl 'Circle → Taifa' at 7.25 — fare+0.25 fee reconciles with SERVICE_FEE_INCL (still a hardcoded client constant, checkout.tsx:113). | Verified: code-walk+API

UserStory: As a rider I want to know whether the fare I'm shown is an official GPRTU rate or a crowdsourced estimate so I can judge how much to trust it
ExpectedBehavior: Next to the drop-off fare, a small pill shows 'official' (green dot) when the resolved SegmentFare.isOfficial is true, or 'estimate' (grey) otherwise; a report-count + freshness caption ('{n} reports · {x}m ago') shows under the fare when reportCount > 0

Worth a pass to confirm the three non-official sources (reported/interpolated/corridor-fallback) aren't visually indistinguishable in a way that misleads riders on data quality. Badge logic verified: green-dot 'official' only when dropoffResult.isOfficial (detail.tsx:651-655), driven by segment_fares.official_fare (segment-fares.ts:61-62); grey 'estimate' otherwise.
