Feature: Fare breakdown — bus fare (VAT-exempt) + service fee + VAT/levies
UserStory: As a passenger I want to see exactly what I'm paying for — the trotro fare vs Troski's service fee and its tax — so the total isn't a mystery
ExpectedBehavior: Fare Breakdown card shows Bus Fare (tagged 'VAT exempt', per Ghana VAT Act 2025), Service Fee (tax-exclusive base), 'VAT + Levies (20%)' line, and a Total; SERVICE_FEE_INCL is a fixed GH₵0.25 tax-inclusive constant split into serviceBase/serviceTax at a 20% TAX_RATE
AcceptanceCriteria: Total shown always equals fare.bus + SERVICE_FEE_INCL exactly (0.25 cedis flat regardless of trip length/fare size); the 'VAT exempt' tag always shows on the Bus Fare row since Ghana's VAT Act exempts public transport; Service Fee + VAT/Levies rows always sum back to exactly GH₵0.25
EdgeCases: A trip with a very small fare (e.g. a short stage fare like the Achimota drop-off) still adds the same flat GH₵0.25 service fee, which is a larger % of the total than on a long trip — worth confirming this is the intended pricing model, not a bug; the 20% TAX_RATE and GH₵0.25 fee are both hardcoded client constants, not fetched from a backend pricing config, so a backend rate change would require an app update to take effect; offline — fare breakdown is computed entirely client-side from already-fetched route/fare data, no network dependency here
TestSteps: 1) Log in with +233200000000, OTP 123456 2) Book the Circle -> Achimota stage (~GH₵4) -> open Fare Breakdown -> confirm Bus Fare ~GH₵4.00 'VAT exempt', Service Fee + VAT/Levies rows sum to GH₵0.25, Total ~GH₵4.25 3) Repeat for Circle -> Taifa full corridor (~GH₵7) -> confirm Total ~GH₵7.25 4) Confirm the service fee (GH₵0.25) is identical in both cases regardless of fare size 5) Cross-check the 20% VAT/Levies split: serviceBase + serviceTax should equal exactly 0.25
Notes: New tracker row for a pricing detail that existed in the code but was never logged in the original tracker. SERVICE_FEE_INCL (GH₵0.25) and TAX_RATE (20%) are hardcoded in checkout.tsx, not sourced from a backend config — flag for backend/finance sign-off that this matches what's actually reconciled server-side. Verified: SERVICE_FEE_INCL=0.25 and TAX_RATE=0.20 hardcoded checkout.tsx:113-114; serviceBase=0.25/1.2, serviceTax=0.25-serviceBase (:115-116) so the two rows sum to exactly ₵0.25 by construction; total=busFare+0.25 (:118); 'VAT exempt' tag always on the Bus Fare row (:351-353); breakdown rendered :348-359 with formatGHS. Flat fee regardless of fare size confirmed (constant — intended model per code comment citing Ghana VAT Act 2025/Act 1151, but % burden on short stage fares is a pricing-policy question). Runtime cross-check: live wallet history shows 'Circle → Taifa' debit of 7.25 = ₵7 fare + ₵0.25 — client constant matches what the backend actually charged. Label nit: '(20%)' is the effective combined VAT+NHIL+GETFund rate on the tax-EXCLUSIVE base; finance sign-off still recommended since a backend rate change requires an app update. | Verified: code-walk+API

Feature: Track your bus — post-booking live GPS + ETA + proximity alerts
UserStory: As a passenger who just booked, I want to see my actual bus moving toward my stop with a live ETA, and get notified as it gets close, so I know when to head out
ExpectedBehavior: Polls vehicle_positions for the booked route every 6s and subscribes to the assigned van's realtime broadcast (gps:van:{vanId}); shows a map with a live bus marker + road-following corridor line to the drop-off, ETA/distance stats, a Data Saver toggle (skips the map, shows a big ETA number instead), and local notifications at ~10min/~5min/arriving thresholds
AcceptanceCriteria: 'Track your bus' only appears on the receipt when route_id or van is present; screen shows an honest 'No bus on this route yet' state when neither the poll nor the realtime broadcast has a position; Data Saver toggle removes all map tiles (data-cost savings) while keeping ETA/plate/distance visible via the sheet; each of the 10min/5min/arriving alerts fires at most once per session (fired ref guards)
EdgeCases: No bus has started the route yet (honest waiting state, not a spinner forever); the assigned van never broadcasts (falls back to whatever vehicle_positions poll returns, or stays in the 'Connecting…' state if a van param was passed but never seen); notification permission denied (alerts silently don't fire, no fallback in-app banner); offline — vehicle_positions poll and realtime channel both fail silently, screen stays on 'No bus'/'Connecting' with no explicit offline messaging to the rider
TestSteps: 1) Log in with +233200000000, OTP 123456, wallet PIN 1234 2) Complete a Circle -> Taifa booking, reach the receipt, tap 'Track your bus' 3) Confirm it opens with either a live bus position or an honest 'No bus on this route yet' state 4) Toggle 'Saver' -> confirm the map disappears and a big ETA number + plate/distance text remain 5) Toggle Saver off -> confirm the map + route line return 6) If a bus is live, confirm the ETA/distance stat row updates over time and that the 'Alert me' switch is on by default
Notes: Brand new screen introduced since the last full tracker pass — not represented anywhere in the original CSV, only implicitly hinted at by the old 'GO Mode' rows for a different flow. This is the primary NEW gap the task asked to check for (booking track screen). Full code-walk: receipt gates the button on route_id||van (receipt.tsx:190-202); track.tsx polls vehicle_positions every 6s (:18,:44-56) + subscribes gps:van broadcast via useRealtimeVehicle(van ?? bus.vanId) with broadcast winning (:60-64); honest states — 'Finding your bus…' while loading, 'No bus on this route yet' with no van, 'Connecting to your bus…' when a van param was passed but never seen (:173-183, matches stated edge case); Data Saver removes the map entirely, big ETA + plate/distance remain (:150-159); road-following corridor line refetched only when >120m off-line (:89-104); proximity alerts fire once each at 10/5/arriving via fired refs (:117-131) with alertsOn default true (:83) and OS permission requested on opt-in (:108-113). Known gaps match EdgeCases: denied notification permission has no in-app fallback banner; no explicit offline messaging. Not runtime-tested with a live bus (no vehicle broadcasting during this pass — same Trotro Pro adoption dependency as L6). | Verified: code-walk

Feature: Driver profile — reviews, ride count, tenure, verification
UserStory: As a passenger I want to see my assigned driver's track record and verification status before I ride so I feel safe
ExpectedBehavior: Driver Details modal (DriverHeader) shows avatar/photo, name, Rides/tenure/Rating stat row (real data from fetchDriverRatingStats/fetchDriverRides), a Verification row (KYC verified vs pending) opening a 'What Troski Verifies' sheet (Driver ID, Photo Check, Safety & Conduct Review), and a real Reviews list from fetchDriverReviews with an honest 'No reviews yet' empty state
AcceptanceCriteria: Driver card on the main checkout screen is only tappable (TouchableOpacity vs plain View) when a real driver name exists; stats show '—'/'New' placeholders instead of fake numbers when rides=0 or rating is null; Reviews list renders real comments+star counts per review, falling back to an honest empty state with a call-to-action framing ('your feedback keeps the fleet accountable') when there are none yet
EdgeCases: Driver assigned but with zero completed rides/ratings yet (shows '—' Rides, 'New' Rating, 'No reviews yet' — never a fabricated number); KYC verified flag not yet set for a real driver (shows 'Verification Pending', tappable to see what's being checked); offline — all driver stats/reviews are fetched once on assigned.driverId change and fail silently (.catch(()=>{})) leaving the sheet stuck on default/empty state
TestSteps: 1) Log in with +233200000000, OTP 123456 2) Book Circle -> Taifa and reach checkout 3) If a real driver is shown on the driver card, tap it -> confirm the Driver Details modal opens 4) Confirm Rides/tenure/Rating reflect real data (or honest placeholders, never obviously-fake numbers) 5) Tap the Verification row -> confirm the 'What Troski Verifies' sheet opens with the three verification items 6) Scroll to Reviews -> confirm either real reviews or the 'No reviews yet' empty state, never a fabricated review
Notes: New tracker row — this driver-profile depth (reviews/tenure/verification sheet) is a meaningful upgrade beyond what the original B1 row described ('Driver card still MOCK') and was never logged on its own. Directly related to the CLAUDE.md 'Trotro Pro' backend note: real data will be sparse until driver adoption grows, which is expected, not a bug. Verified: driver card tappable only when a real driverName exists (checkout.tsx:291-294); Driver Details modal :421-461 with DriverHeader :502-536 — Rides shows '—' when 0 (:519), Rating 'New' when null (:521), tenure 'New' under 1yr (:506); Verification row reflects kyc_status approved/verified (fleet.ts:47, checkout.tsx:530-534) and opens the 'What Troski Verifies' sheet with the three VERIFY_ITEMS (:30-34,:464-488); Reviews from fetchDriverReviews via RLS-safe driver_reviews view (driver-ratings.ts:59-72) with the honest 'No reviews yet — your feedback keeps the fleet accountable' empty state (:451-457); all fetches fail silently to defaults (offline = stuck-on-default, as documented). Real data sparse until Trotro Pro adoption — expected. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want to see my coin balance and tier progress at a glance
ExpectedBehavior: Coins tab shows a semicircular speedometer gauge that counts up the balance and sweeps an arc from the current tier's min_points to the next tier's min_points, with a gold TroskiCoin marker riding the tip and 11 ticks lighting up as progress increases.
AcceptanceCriteria: Gauge target uses Math.max(1, levelMax-levelMin) so denominator can never be 0 (rewards.tsx:191); top tier (levelMax=null) renders the arc full and shows 'MAX' label; Tier pill + Tier Journey strip both read from the same LEVELS/current_level as the gauge so labels never disagree; Today/Streak/Rank stat row taps Rank to push /leaderboard.
EdgeCases: Brand-new account with 0 points (gauge starts at 0, no divide-by-zero); user already at top tier 'Troski Legend' (levelMax null, arc must show full not empty); pull-to-refresh while gauge count-up animation is still running; rank shows '--' when userRank is null (no leaderboard row yet).
TestSteps: 1) Login with +233200000000 / OTP 123456 2) Open the Rewards tab (bottom nav) 3) Confirm the Coins sub-tab is selected by default 4) Watch the gauge count up from 0 to the real balance with the coin marker riding the arc 5) Confirm the tier pill emoji/name matches the Tier Journey strip's current (bounce) node 6) Tap the Rank stat and confirm navigation to /leaderboard 7) Pull to refresh and confirm gauge re-animates without crashing
Notes: Gauge denominator Math.max(1, levelMax-levelMin) confirmed at rewards.tsx:191; top tier levelMax=null renders arc full (target=1, rewards.tsx:189-190) with 'MAX' scale label (rewards.tsx:276); tier pill, gauge and TierJourney all read the same levelSlug/LEVELS (rewards.tsx:432-434, 572-574, 615); Rank stat renders '--' when rank null and pushes /leaderboard on tap (rewards.tsx:610); pull-to-refresh re-runs the rAF count-up via useEffect deps (rewards.tsx:174-186). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want to see my streak and get warned before I lose it
ExpectedBehavior: Earn tab shows a gradient Daily Streak card with a 7-day week grid anchored to the real weekday (Mon-start), lit days rendered as coins, an 'at risk' note with an hours-left countdown when the user hasn't reported today, and a bonus-earned message right after hitting the STREAK_CONFIG.THRESHOLD_DAYS multiple.
AcceptanceCriteria: Week grid isDayDone(i) uses last_report_date compared to today's YYYY-MM-DD string so only real reported days light up (rewards.tsx:441-443); streakAtRisk is only true when streak>0 AND !reportedToday; hoursLeft is Math.max(1, ...) so it never shows 0h or negative; bonusJustDone fires only when streak%THRESHOLD_DAYS===0 and streak>0.
EdgeCases: User with streak=0 (no urgency copy, no coins shown in grid); user who already reported today (no at-risk banner even with a live streak); streak exactly on a 7-day multiple (bonus copy shown, not at-risk copy); app opened right after midnight rollover (hoursLeft should reset near 24h).
TestSteps: 1) Login and open Rewards → Earn tab 2) Note today's weekday dot and whether it shows a coin (reported) or a number 3) If streak>0 and not reported today, confirm the frosted note reads '...report within Xh to keep it!' 4) Submit any report (e.g. Report a fare) to bump today's report 5) Return to Rewards → Earn and confirm today's dot now shows a coin and urgency copy disappears
Notes: isDayDone compares last_report_date to today's YYYY-MM-DD key (rewards.tsx:440-443); streakAtRisk = streak>0 && !reportedToday (rewards.tsx:448); hoursLeft floored at 1 via Math.max (rewards.tsx:444-447) and resets near 24h after midnight; bonusJustDone = streak%7===0 && streak>0 (rewards.tsx:450) and wins over at-risk copy in the ternary (rewards.tsx:723-728); streak=0 shows the neutral 'Complete 7 days...' copy. Minor: non-reported days always show sequence numbers 1-7 rather than dates (cosmetic). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want clear ways to earn coins
ExpectedBehavior: Earn tab renders a 2-column grid of 6 mission cards (Fare, Queue, Incident, Pulse, 7-day streak, Refer) each showing a real point value pulled from REPORT_POINTS/STREAK_CONFIG/REFERRAL_POINTS, and tapping a card routes to the matching report screen or jumps to the Referrals/Earn sub-tab.
AcceptanceCriteria: Every card's '+N' badge is a live constant (lib/constants/rewards.ts), not a hardcoded string; tapping 'Report a fare'/'Queue status'/'Report incidents'/'Share to Pulse' pushes the correct report/* route; tapping '7-day streak' (route:null) stays on Earn tab; tapping 'Refer a friend' switches to the Referrals sub-tab with a selection haptic.
EdgeCases: Tapping a mission card while offline (router.push still fires, report screen must show its own network error); rapid double-tap on a mission card (no guard against double navigation); emoji rendering on Android devices without full emoji font coverage.
TestSteps: 1) Login and open Rewards → Earn tab 2) Tap 'Report a fare' and confirm navigation to /report/fare 3) Go back, tap 'Refer a friend' and confirm the Referrals sub-tab becomes active 4) Go back to Earn, tap '7-day streak' card and confirm it stays on the Earn tab (no dead navigation)
Notes: All 6 mission point badges are live constants (rewards.tsx:494-501 reading REPORT_POINTS fare=10/queue=5/incident=15/tale=8, STREAK_CONFIG.BONUS_POINTS=5, REFERRAL_POINTS=500 from lib/constants/rewards.ts); goEarn pushes /report/fare|queue|incident|photo, route:null stays on Earn, 'tab:Referrals' switches sub-tab with Haptics.selectionAsync (rewards.tsx:526-531). Confirmed no double-tap navigation guard (edge acknowledged, not a defect). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want to share my referral code with friends
ExpectedBehavior: Referrals sub-tab shows the user's referral_code (auto-generated as TROSKI-XXXX on first load if missing) with Copy (clipboard, checkmark for 1.8s) and Share (system share sheet) actions, plus a live count of completed referrals.
AcceptanceCriteria: Code auto-provisions and persists to contributor_profiles.referral_code exactly once per user (rewards.tsx:374-378); Copy falls back to Share if Clipboard throws; referralCount is read from a head-count query on the referrals table so it reflects real completed signups, not an optimistic local counter; components/ReferralCard.tsx is unused dead code (logic is inlined in this tab per CLAUDE.md) — do not confuse it with the live UI.
EdgeCases: No referral_code column yet on a fresh profile (must generate+persist, not crash); Clipboard permission denied (must fall back to Share silently); referralCount = 0 (copy reads 'No referrals yet' in the History alert); Share sheet cancelled by user (no error surfaced).
TestSteps: 1) Login and open Rewards → Referrals sub-tab 2) Confirm a code like TROSKI-XXXX renders (not '—') 3) Tap the copy icon, confirm the checkmark appears then reverts after ~2s 4) Tap Share and confirm the system share sheet opens with the referral message 5) Tap 'View History' and confirm the alert shows the real referral count
Notes: Auto-provision TROSKI-XXXX from deviceId prefix and single update to contributor_profiles when referral_code missing (rewards.tsx:374-378); Copy catch falls back to handleShare (rewards.tsx:503-513); referralCount from head-count query on referrals by referrer_device_id (rewards.tsx:380-384); 'No referrals yet' History alert copy confirmed (rewards.tsx:831); components/ReferralCard.tsx confirmed dead code (grep: zero importers). Minor: code is deviceId-derived not random, so it never rotates. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want to see my coin activity history
ExpectedBehavior: History sub-tab shows a Total Coins card, This Month/Redeemed summary cards computed from points_history, and the full history grouped into Today/Yesterday/Earlier with a type-specific icon and signed +/- point value per row.
AcceptanceCriteria: Grouping (derived.groups) is computed client-side from real created_at timestamps, not backend-paginated, so it always reflects the freshest refetch; historyMeta() maps report_type to a label+icon (fare/queue/incident/train/tale/referral/streak) and falls back to 'Coins Earned'/'Coin Redeemed' for unknown/negative entries; redeemed total only increments for negative-point rows.
EdgeCases: Empty history (shows 'No coin activity yet' empty state, not a blank list); an entry with an unrecognized report_type (must fall back gracefully, not crash); entry with points<0 and no reason (falls back to 'Coin Redeemed'); month boundary edge (entry from last day of previous month must not count toward monthEarned).
TestSteps: 1) Login and open Rewards → History sub-tab 2) Confirm Total Coins matches the Coins tab balance 3) Submit a fare report to generate a new history entry 4) Refresh and confirm a new 'Today' group row appears with the correct icon and +N value 5) Confirm 'This Month' total increased by the same amount
Notes: Everything else holds: Today/Yesterday/Earlier grouping from real created_at (rewards.tsx:464-482), historyMeta falls back to reason/'Coins Earned'/'Coin Redeemed' for unknown or negative entries (rewards.tsx:328-340), redeemed only sums points<0, month boundary uses monthStart>= check, empty state renders (rewards.tsx:785-789). Fix = paginate or raise limit and/or compute summaries server-side. | Verified: code-walk | Fixed+retested 2026-07-04 (code-verified, typecheck clean)

UserStory: As a commuter I want to track which achievements I've earned
ExpectedBehavior: Coins tab renders a badge grid (all badges from the badges table) with earned badges shown full-color and unearned badges dimmed to 45% opacity, plus an 'X of Y' earned counter.
AcceptanceCriteria: Badge grid only renders when allBadges.length>0 (no empty grid flash); earned state is computed by matching earnedBadges (user's actual unlocked badges) against allBadges by id, not by name; icon/color come from BADGE_ICONS/BADGE_COLORS maps shared with app/profile/[deviceId].tsx Trophy Case so both screens render identical badge art.
EdgeCases: User with zero earned badges (all cards dimmed, counter reads '0 of Y'); a badge row whose icon/color string isn't in the lookup maps (falls back to Star/#F59E0B, doesn't crash); badges table temporarily empty (section doesn't render at all rather than showing an empty grid).
TestSteps: 1) Login and open Rewards → Coins tab 2) Scroll to the Badges section 3) Confirm the '# of #' counter matches the number of full-color cards 4) Confirm unearned badges are visibly dimmed vs earned ones 5) Tap a badge card (non-interactive is expected — no crash on tap)
Notes: Badge section gated on allBadges.length>0 (rewards.tsx:657); earned computed by id match earnedBadges.some(e=>e.id===b.id) (rewards.tsx:665); unearned dimmed opacity 0.45 (rewards.tsx:669); icon/color fall back to Star/#F59E0B (rewards.tsx:666-667); 'X of Y' counter (rewards.tsx:661); BADGE_ICONS/BADGE_COLORS maps mirror profile Trophy Case (rewards.tsx:77-85). Live badges table rows return icon/color values ('star'/'amber' etc.) all present in the lookup maps. | Verified: code-walk+API

UserStory: As a commuter I want to see my weekly rank against other riders
ExpectedBehavior: Full-screen weekly leaderboard fetches entries ordered by weekly_points descending, shows medal emoji for top 3 and '#N' for the rest, highlights the current user's row, and shows 'Your rank: #N' in the header when the user is ranked.
AcceptanceCriteria: fetchLeaderboard query now has an explicit .order('weekly_points', { ascending: false }) so on-screen rank always matches point order; current user's row is detected via device_id===deviceId and gets an amber border highlight + '(You)' suffix; pull-to-refresh re-runs the same ordered query.
EdgeCases: Fresh week with zero reports from anyone (empty state 'No leaderboard data yet'); user not yet in the leaderboard view (userRank stays null, header sub-line hidden); tie in weekly_points between two users (order is stable but rank number is still index+1, so ties don't share a rank); user's own row scrolled off-screen (highlight border still applied, just not visible without scrolling).
TestSteps: 1) Login and navigate to Rewards → Coins tab → View Leaderboard 2) Confirm the list is sorted highest-to-lowest by points (top row has the highest pts) 3) Confirm your own row (if present) has the amber highlight border and '(You)' label 4) Pull to refresh and confirm the order is unchanged / still correctly sorted
ErrorsFound: CRITICAL (fixed): fetchLeaderboard previously had no .order() clause, so rows came back in unsorted DB order and on-screen ranks (#1, #2, #3...) did not correspond to real weekly_points — a user with fewer points could outrank one with more.
Notes: Re-confirmed this pass: .order('weekly_points', {ascending:false}) present at lib/services/rewards.ts:524-528 (limit 50) and rank=index+1; live REST GET with the same order clause returns 200 and sorted rows; row highlight via device_id===deviceId + '(You)' suffix (app/leaderboard.tsx:40-45, 59-62); medals for top 3 (leaderboard.tsx:47-50); pull-to-refresh re-runs refetch; empty state present. Caveat: current week has all weekly_points=0, so displayed order among ties is arbitrary-but-stable (ties don't share a rank); still one fetch per mount, no realtime. | Verified: code-walk+API

UserStory: As a commuter I want a fun celebration when I hit a milestone
ExpectedBehavior: On tier-up or hitting a 7-day-multiple streak, the Coins tab fires a confetti burst plus a success haptic exactly once per milestone, tracked via an AsyncStorage de-dupe key so re-opening the tab doesn't replay it.
AcceptanceCriteria: Celebration state (@troski_rewards_celebrated_v1) stores both {level, streak} so a tier-up and a streak-milestone are each recorded independently across app restarts; confetti only mounts while `celebrating` is true and unmounts itself via onAnimationEnd; Haptics.notificationAsync(Success) fires alongside the visual.
EdgeCases: User hits a tier-up AND a streak-multiple in the same profile update (only one celebration fires per mount since they share the same celebrating boolean — the other milestone is marked 'seen' without ever showing); app force-closed mid-animation (AsyncStorage key already written before the animation finishes, so re-open won't replay even though the user didn't see it); first-ever app open for a new account (seen defaults to 'passenger'/0 so no false celebration fires for level 1).
TestSteps: 1) Use a test account close to a tier boundary (or manipulate points via reports) 2) Cross the tier threshold with a report submission 3) Open/refresh the Rewards tab and confirm confetti + haptic fire once 4) Force-close and reopen the app, return to Rewards, and confirm confetti does NOT replay for the same tier-up
Notes: AsyncStorage key @troski_rewards_celebrated_v1 stores {level, streak} independently (rewards.tsx:75, 392-418); seen defaults to 'passenger'/0 so no false celebration for new accounts (rewards.tsx:397-399); confetti mounts only while celebrating and clears via onAnimationEnd (rewards.tsx:900-913); Haptics Success fires alongside (rewards.tsx:408). Known quirks match edge notes exactly: simultaneous tier-up+streak share one burst, and the seen key is written before the animation completes (force-close mid-animation forfeits the replay). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want a community feed of transit moments
ExpectedBehavior: Pulse tab shows a paginated feed of posts (author avatar/badge, location, caption or media, like/comment/share/save actions) with pull-to-refresh and infinite scroll, plus a Threads-style compose bar for text or camera posts.
AcceptanceCriteria: Text-only posts render in a distinct card style (textPostCard) instead of the media layout; video posts show a play-button thumbnail that opens app/reel.tsx instead of inline autoplay; double-tap on media triggers the like animation via the shared DoubleTapLike gesture; onEndReached triggers loadMore only when hasMore is true.
EdgeCases: Empty feed (shows 'No posts yet' with a 'Post to Pulse' CTA, not a blank screen); offline load (initial fetch fails — feed should not crash, ideally shows cached/empty state); a post with no caption and no media edge case; rapid double-tap already-liked post (should not toggle to unliked via the double-tap gesture, only single-tap does).
TestSteps: 1) Login and open the Pulse tab 2) Confirm the compose bar, feed cards (or empty state) render 3) Pull to refresh and confirm the RefreshControl spinner appears then clears 4) Scroll to the bottom of a long feed and confirm more posts load (or the ActivityIndicator footer appears) 5) Toggle airplane mode, pull to refresh, and confirm no crash occurs
Notes: Text posts render the distinct textPostCard (TalesScreen.tsx:227-242); video posts show play overlay + duration badge and push /reel (TalesScreen.tsx:248-274, 453-460); DoubleTapLike only likes when !localLiked so double-tap never un-likes (TalesScreen.tsx:146-151); onEndReached passed only when hasMore (TalesScreen.tsx:515) with spinner footer; empty state has 'Post to Pulse' CTA (TalesScreen.tsx:494-506). Flag: offline pull-to-refresh doesn't crash but fetchTales returns [] on error (lib/services/tales.ts:124-127) so refresh REPLACES the loaded feed with the empty state instead of keeping cached posts — confusing offline behavior worth a follow-up. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a commuter I want to post a photo/video or text update
ExpectedBehavior: Compose screen supports a text-only mode (?mode=text) or photo/video mode (up to 10 images or a 60s video), requires a location and a caption up to 280 chars, awards +15 XP (REPORT_POINTS.tale) on success, and shows upload progress for media.
AcceptanceCriteria: Caption input is hard-capped at 280 characters; location is a required field before submit is enabled; video capture/attach is capped at 60 seconds; successful submit awards exactly REPORT_POINTS.tale points and appears immediately at the top of the Pulse feed on return.
EdgeCases: Video exceeding 60s (must be rejected/trimmed, not silently accepted); no location permission granted (location required — submit should stay disabled with a clear prompt); network drop mid-upload (progress bar should show a failure state, not hang forever); video thumbnail generation failing silently (post still saves but shows a blank thumbnail in-feed).
TestSteps: 1) Login, open Pulse tab, tap the camera icon (compose bar) 2) Attach a photo, add a caption and confirm the location field 3) Submit and confirm a success toast/points award (+15) 4) Return to the Pulse feed and confirm the new post appears at the top 5) Repeat via the text-only entry ('What's happening on your route?') and confirm a text-styled card posts correctly
Notes: Also: tracker AC says +15 XP but REPORT_POINTS.tale=8 (lib/constants/rewards.ts:8) — success alert shows the real awarded value (photo.tsx:197-201) so the app is honest, the tracker copy is wrong; location/caption are validated on press via alerts (photo.tsx:156-174), the submit button is never disabled for missing fields (AC wording 'submit stays disabled' does not match implementation); 280-char cap enforced by slice (photo.tsx:390); >60s gallery videos are NOT re-validated after selection — videoMaxDuration on launchImageLibraryAsync (photo.tsx:114-119) is not enforced on Android library picks, and there is no asset.duration check; video upload progress bar present (photo.tsx:287-292) but a mid-upload network drop leaves it stuck (no failure state on the bar, only the terminal error alert). | Verified: code-walk | Fixed+retested 2026-07-04 (code-verified, typecheck clean)

UserStory: As a commuter I want an immersive TikTok-style view of a video post
ExpectedBehavior: Tapping a video post opens a fullscreen looping muted-by-default video with like (spring animation), comment (hands off to the feed's CommentSheet via DeviceEventEmitter), follow, share, and mute-toggle controls, and pauses playback when the screen loses focus.
AcceptanceCriteria: Video is paused via useFocusEffect's cleanup on blur/unmount (player.pause()) so it does not keep decoding/burning battery+data in the background — this was a real fix, not just intended behavior; tapping Comment emits 'openComment' with the postId and navigates back to the feed, which opens CommentSheet after a 300ms delay; Follow button is hidden entirely when viewing your own post (isOwnPost check).
EdgeCases: Like toggled rapidly (optimistic local state can desync from server if addReaction/removeReaction fails — code does roll back on failure); navigating to the reel with a missing/invalid videoUrl param (VideoView + useVideoPlayer given an empty string); tapping mute mid-scrub; backgrounding the app while a reel is open (useFocusEffect + AppState — only focus-loss via navigation is explicitly handled, not OS backgrounding).
TestSteps: 1) Login, open Pulse tab, tap a video post's thumbnail to open the reel 2) Confirm the video autoplays muted and loops 3) Tap the like heart and confirm the spring bounce + count increment 4) Tap the music-disk to unmute, confirm audio plays 5) Tap Comment and confirm you're returned to the feed with the CommentSheet open for that post 6) Tap the dismiss handle to close and confirm the video is not audible in the background afterward
ErrorsFound: FIXED (verified): video previously kept decoding in the background after the reel was dismissed/backgrounded (no pause-on-blur), burning battery and mobile data; now paused via useFocusEffect cleanup.

UserStory: As a commuter I want a combined feed of my activity and alerts
ExpectedBehavior: Segmented Notifications/Activity screen: Notifications tab shows alert cards (unread indicator, mark-all-read) that refetch on focus; Activity tab groups the user's own reports/trips by date (Today/Yesterday/This Week/Older) with swipe-to-delete and infinite scroll.
AcceptanceCriteria: Notifications refetch via useRefreshOnFocus so returning to the tab shows freshly read/unread state; swiping an Activity row fully open auto-triggers delete (onSwipeableOpen) in addition to the explicit Delete button; loadMore on Activity only fires after the user has actually scrolled (hasScrolledRef guard) to avoid firing on initial mount; 'Mark all read' / 'Clear all' header action only appears in the segment it applies to.
EdgeCases: Empty notifications ('All caught up' empty state); empty activity ('Keep the pulse.' empty state, distinct copy from Notifications); swiping a row while a delete is already animating (LayoutAnimation on Android has known flakiness per project notes); tapping a notification with no linkTo (marks read but doesn't navigate, verify no crash).
TestSteps: 1) Login and open the Activity tab (labelled 'Activity' in the tab bar) 2) On the Notifications segment, confirm unread items show a left accent bar and the count badge, then tap 'Mark all read' and confirm the badge clears 3) Switch to the Activity segment, confirm items are grouped by date 4) Swipe an Activity row left past the threshold and confirm it deletes 5) Scroll to the bottom of a long Activity list and confirm more items load
Notes: Rest verified: notifications refetch on focus (activity.tsx:203 useRefreshOnFocus); onSwipeableOpen auto-delete (activity.tsx:162) plus explicit Delete; hasScrolledRef guard (activity.tsx:205-208, 411); 'Mark all read'/'Clear all' correctly per-segment (activity.tsx:295-303); distinct empty states (activity.tsx:350/389); no-linkTo notification just marks read (activity.tsx:266-269). NEEDS UX REVIEW: feed is GLOBAL — fetchRecentActivity has no device/user filter (activity.ts:30-90), contradicting the 'my own reports/trips' user story; prior concerns stand (dismiss is AsyncStorage client-only, hardcoded type→icon map, Android LayoutAnimation flakiness). | Verified: code-walk | Fixed+retested 2026-07-04 (code-verified, typecheck clean)

UserStory: As a commuter I want official transit announcements from GPRTU/GRDA/partners
ExpectedBehavior: Bulletin screen lists official_announcements (published only) ordered newest-first with source badges (GPRTU/GRDA/GRA/MMTL/MOT/TROSKI), category filter chips, an urgent/important marker for high/urgent priority items, and refetches on screen focus.
AcceptanceCriteria: Query is scoped to is_published=true and limited to 100 rows, newest first; category filter chips (All/Fares/Routes/Strikes/Policy/Notices/Insights) filter the already-fetched list client-side with no extra round-trip; urgent/high priority items get a red badge + left accent border; useFocusEffect re-runs load() every time the screen regains focus so it doesn't go stale after first mount.
EdgeCases: Empty announcements table ('No announcements yet' empty state); a Supabase query error (caught, logs a warning, and falls back to an empty array rather than crashing); more than 100 published announcements (list silently caps at 100, no pagination or 'load more'); filtering to a category with zero matches after the base list loaded (empty state text is generic, doesn't mention the active filter).
TestSteps: 1) Login and navigate to Bulletin (from wherever it's linked, e.g. a menu/notification) 2) Confirm the list loads with source badges and category labels 3) Tap a category filter chip (e.g. 'Strikes') and confirm the list narrows to matching items only 4) Tap 'All' to reset 5) Background the app, return to the screen, and confirm it refetches (pull-to-refresh also works)
Notes: Query scoped is_published=true, published_at desc, limit 100 (app/bulletin/index.tsx:96-102); error path warns + falls back to [] (bulletin:104-107); category chips filter client-side via useMemo, no round-trip (bulletin:123-126); urgent/high get red URGENT/IMPORTANT badge + left accent border (bulletin:132,135,145-151); useFocusEffect re-runs load() on focus (bulletin:115); live GET confirms published rows exist with source/category/priority matching the meta maps. Confirmed edges: >100 rows silently capped (no pagination) and the filtered-empty state text is generic ('No announcements yet') without naming the active filter — confusing copy, minor. | Verified: code-walk+API

Feature: Comment on a Pulse post (with threaded replies)
UserStory: As a commuter I want to comment on a post and reply to other comments
ExpectedBehavior: Tapping the comment icon on a Pulse post or in the reel opens a bottom sheet listing comments (author, level emoji, timestamp, 'You' badge on own comments), a 280-char input to post a new comment or a reply, and a 'View N replies' toggle per comment that lazy-loads replies.
AcceptanceCriteria: Comment input is capped at 280 characters (maxLength + slice guard); posting is disabled while isPosting is true or the trimmed text is empty; replying to a comment shows a dismissible 'Replying to {name}' indicator above the input and auto-expands that comment's replies on successful post; toggling 'View replies' only calls loadReplies() the first time (repliesMap cache prevents refetching every toggle).
EdgeCases: Posting a comment while offline (addComment should fail gracefully, not silently lose the typed text); a comment thread with 0 replies (no 'View replies' row shown at all); rapid double-tap on Post button (isPosting guard should prevent a duplicate comment); closing the sheet mid-reply (replyTarget/text state should reset, not leak into the next post opened).
TestSteps: 1) Login, open the Pulse tab, tap the comment icon on any post 2) Type a comment and tap Post, confirm it appears at the top/bottom of the list immediately 3) Tap 'Reply' on an existing comment, confirm the 'Replying to...' indicator appears, type and send a reply 4) Confirm 'View 1 reply' appears under that comment and expands to show it 5) Close the sheet and reopen on a different post, confirm no leftover reply-target text from the previous post
Notes: Everything else verified: 280-char cap via maxLength + slice (CommentSheet.tsx:195,200); Post disabled while isPosting or empty trim (CommentSheet.tsx:203-210); 'Replying to {name}' dismissible indicator (CommentSheet.tsx:172-184); successful reply auto-expands the parent's replies (CommentSheet.tsx:52-56); repliesMap caches so 'View replies' fetches once (CommentSheet.tsx:71-73, useComments.ts:25-42); zero-reply comments show no toggle (CommentSheet.tsx:94); offline post fails gracefully and keeps the typed text (text cleared only on success, CommentSheet.tsx:51-57). | Verified: code-walk | Fixed+retested 2026-07-04 (code-verified, typecheck clean)

UserStory: As a commuter I want to flag content that violates community guidelines
ExpectedBehavior: Tapping the '⋯' menu on a post and choosing Report shows a confirm alert, then upserts a row into content_reports (reporter_device_id, content_type='tale', content_id, reason='inappropriate') and shows a 'Reported' confirmation regardless of outcome.
AcceptanceCriteria: Confirm alert requires an explicit 'Report' tap (Cancel is the default/safe option); upsert uses onConflict 'reporter_device_id,content_type,content_id' so the same user reporting the same post twice does not create duplicate rows; success alert text is shown even if the Supabase call throws (caught and swallowed) — the user always sees a 'Thanks for helping keep Troski Pulse safe' confirmation, which means a silent backend failure is invisible to both user and QA.
EdgeCases: Reporting the same post twice from the same device (should upsert/no-op on the conflict key, not duplicate); reporting while offline (call throws, but UI still claims success — masks real failures); reporting your own post (menu shows Report even for isOwn posts alongside Delete — verify both options coexist correctly); Supabase RLS rejecting the insert (same silent-success problem).
TestSteps: 1) Login, open the Pulse tab, tap '⋯' on any post (not your own) 2) Tap Report, confirm the alert, and confirm a 'Reported' success message appears 3) Repeat Report on the same post a second time and confirm no crash/duplicate error 4) Toggle airplane mode and repeat Report, confirm the UI still shows success (note as a known gap, not a pass/fail on UX honesty) 5) Check Supabase content_reports table (if accessible) to confirm rows were actually written for the online attempts
Notes: Verified alongside: confirm alert with Cancel default before reporting (TalesScreen.tsx:422-424); upsert onConflict 'reporter_device_id,content_type,content_id' dedupes repeat reports (TalesScreen.tsx:426-431); own posts show Report and Delete together in the menu as designed (TalesScreen.tsx:212-221); anon read-probe of content_reports returned 200/[] so client insert path is at least reachable. Fix = check the {error} result and show a distinct failure/queued message. | Verified: code-walk+API | Fixed+retested 2026-07-04 (code-verified, typecheck clean)

EdgeCases: Traffic API returning empty array shows 'Traffic data unavailable... Pull to retry' empty state; traffic_condition null falls back to busyness.level via BUSYNESS_TO_COND map; 'Flowing' label only shows when busyness.level==='low' — otherwise shows '{label} now' even for moderate/heavy
TestSteps: 1) Home -> Services -> Queue -> Traffic link, or deep-link /traffic/status 2) Confirm stat chips (Heavy/Moderate/Clear/Total) match the corridor list below 3) Confirm corridors are sorted worst-first 4) Pull to refresh 5) Tap back (glass button) and confirm navigation returns cleanly
Notes: Old KnownConcern ('Flowing' label doesn't change for moderate/heavy) confirmed still present in code (metaRow logic only special-cases busyness==='low'). No per-corridor detail drill-down exists, matching old note. Re-verified: 5-min staleTime (app/traffic/status.tsx:46), worst-first rank (status.tsx:49-52), BUSYNESS_TO_COND fallback (status.tsx:29-37), 'Traffic data unavailable' empty state (status.tsx:131), 'Flowing' only when busyness==='low' else '{label} now' (status.tsx:170). Live GET https://www.troski.me/api/traffic/summary returned 200 with routes[]; all sampled rows had traffic_condition:null so the busyness fallback is the ACTIVE production path right now. | Verified: code-walk+API

UserStory: As a rider I want station queue levels so I know where the shortest wait is
ExpectedBehavior: Queue status list shows the latest report per station, busiest-first, flags stale data, and lets a rider jump straight into reporting
AcceptanceCriteria: fetchQueueStatus returns latest-per-station queue reports, busiest-first, with liveNow/busy/nearFull/total stat chips; stale reports (>2h, via isStale) flagged red in the age label; capacity bar visualizes QUEUE_META level; footer 'Report a Queue' CTA -> report/queue
EdgeCases: Zero queue reports shows 'No queue reports yet' empty state with CTA hint; stale (>2h) reports show 'Stale · {time ago}' in red instead of hiding; no per-station detail/drill-down exists (list only)
TestSteps: 1) Home -> Services -> Queue, or deep-link /queue/status 2) Confirm stat chips (Live Now/Busy/Near Full/Total) 3) Confirm stations sorted busiest-first with capacity bars 4) Tap 'Traffic' pill in the header -> confirms nav to /traffic/status 5) Tap 'Report a Queue' footer CTA -> confirms nav to report/queue
Notes: Old KnownConcern (status colors assume SQL strings; no per-station detail) — QUEUE_META keys match the DB status strings directly (empty/short/moderate/long/very_long) so this is a real but low-risk coupling; no per-station detail screen exists, confirmed. Re-verified: latest-per-station busiest-first (lib/services/queueStatus.ts:41,77), isStale >2h (queueStatus.ts:26,72), unknown status strings skipped not crashed (queueStatus.ts:63 — softens the SQL-string coupling concern), 'Stale · x' red label (app/queue/status.tsx:116-117), 'No queue reports yet' (status.tsx:102), Traffic pill -> /traffic/status (status.tsx:71), footer CTA -> report/queue (status.tsx:148). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a rider I want stations on a map so I can browse the network visually
ExpectedBehavior: Full-screen station map with queue-colored dots, an optional OSM transport overlay, and a synced bottom-sheet list that supports sorting, search, and tap-to-focus
AcceptanceCriteria: Station dots colored by queue status (QUEUE_COLORS/QUEUE_SEVERITY); OSM transport-stop/corridor overlay toggle (Layers button); bottom sheet station list with Nearest/Shortest sort tabs + search; tapping a station or route zooms/tilts the camera and opens the matching card
EdgeCases: getDistance() always returns null (Phase 1 — no GPS-based distance despite 'Nearest' tab label); transport layer route/stop rendering depends on useTransportData hook succeeding — no explicit error/empty state if that OSM dataset fails to load; search has no fuzzy matching or city filter
TestSteps: 1) Home -> Lines/Routes -> or deep-link /stations 2) Confirm map renders colored station dots + toggle Layers button shows/hides OSM transport overlay 3) Tap a station dot -> confirm camera zooms/tilts and bottom sheet snaps + scrolls to the matching card 4) Tap a route corridor line -> confirm RouteInfoCard overlay appears 5) Use the search box in the bottom sheet to filter stations by name
Notes: Old KnownConcern (Distance null Phase 1; 60s stale; search no fuzzy/city) confirmed: getDistance() hardcoded to return null (app/stations/index.tsx:214-216). Re-verified: getDistance hardcoded null Phase 1 (app/stations/index.tsx:213-216 with explicit comment), 'Nearest' tab actually sorts major-first/alphabetical (index.tsx:107) — label overpromises, flag as confusing copy; QUEUE_COLORS/QUEUE_SEVERITY dots (index.tsx:28,36,46), Layers toggle (index.tsx:743), no explicit error state if useTransportData OSM load fails, confirmed. | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a driver I want to register my role/vehicle so I can track my trips
ExpectedBehavior: First-time visitors are prompted to register a driver/mate profile before the dashboard unlocks
AcceptanceCriteria: On first load with no existing profile, auto-opens registration card: name + role (Driver/Mate) + optional vehicle number -> registerDriver(deviceId, name, role, vehicle) creates the driver profile and reveals the dashboard
EdgeCases: Empty name keeps 'Get Started' disabled; role is fixed at registration time with no edit path visible on this screen (immutable per old note); no document/ID verification of any kind before granting driver status
TestSteps: 1) Deep-link to /driver (no Driver tab in nav) 2) On first visit, confirm the registration card auto-appears 3) Enter a name, pick Driver or Mate, optionally a vehicle number 4) Tap Get Started -> confirm dashboard replaces the registration card 5) Force-quit and reopen -> confirm profile persists (no re-registration prompt)
Notes: No functional change from old row (No doc verification; role immutable) — confirmed no edit-role UI exists anywhere in this file. Re-verified: registerDriver(deviceId,name,role,vehicle) (app/driver/index.tsx:96; lib/services/driver.ts:46), 'Get Started' disabled on empty name (index.tsx:197), no role-edit UI anywhere in file, no doc/ID verification (anyone becomes 'driver' instantly — flag for owner). | Verified: code-walk

UserStory: As a driver I want to log trips/earnings so I can track my income
ExpectedBehavior: Driver logs individual trips via a modal and sees period-scoped stats (trips/earnings/passengers/avg fare) update immediately
AcceptanceCriteria: Today/Week/Month period tabs drive getDriverTrips(deviceId, period); stats grid (Trips/Earnings/Passengers/Avg Fare); 'Log a Trip' modal with From/To (+ quick station chips from usePopularStations), passengers + fare inputs -> logTrip records with current timestamp; recent trips list refreshes after logging
EdgeCases: From/To required to enable 'Log Trip' (disabled otherwise); passengers/fare default to 0 via parseInt/parseFloat fallback if left blank or non-numeric; no way to edit or delete a logged trip once submitted; departed_at is always 'now' — no backdating for trips logged after the fact
TestSteps: 1) On the Driver dashboard, switch between Today/Week/Month tabs and confirm stats recompute 2) Tap 'Log a Trip' 3) Fill From/To (or tap a quick station chip) + passengers + fare 4) Tap Log Trip -> confirm modal closes and the new trip appears at the top of Recent Trips with correct fare 5) Confirm stats grid (Trips/Earnings) updates to include the new trip
Notes: No functional change from old row (Current timestamp only; fare no validation; can't edit/delete) — all three still true in code (no min/max fare check, no edit/delete handlers anywhere in the file). Re-verified: period tabs -> getDriverTrips date windows (app/driver/index.tsx:69; driver.ts:83-98), parseInt/parseFloat ||0 fallbacks (index.tsx:111-112), Log Trip disabled without From/To (index.tsx:382), no edit/delete handlers, departed_at always now — no backdating, and fare has NO upper bound (unlike rider fare report's 200 cap) — flag missing validation. | Verified: code-walk
